class MyCircularDeque:

    def __init__(self, k: int):
        """
        Initialize your data structure here. Set the maximum size of the deque to be k.
        """
        self.data = [0] * k
        self.capacity = k
        self.front = 0  # Index of the first element
        self.rear = 0   # Index where the next element will be inserted from the rear
        self.size = 0   # Current number of elements in the deque

    def insertFront(self, value: int) -> bool:
        """
        Adds an item at the front of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful.
        """
        if self.isFull():
            return False
        
        # Decrement front pointer circularly
        self.front = (self.front - 1 + self.capacity) % self.capacity
        self.data[self.front] = value
        self.size += 1
        return True

    def insertLast(self, value: int) -> bool:
        """
        Adds an item at the rear of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful.
        """
        if self.isFull():
            return False
        
        self.data[self.rear] = value
        # Increment rear pointer circularly
        self.rear = (self.rear + 1) % self.capacity
        self.size += 1
        return True

    def deleteFront(self) -> bool:
        """
        Deletes an item from the front of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful.
        """
        if self.isEmpty():
            return False
        
        # Increment front pointer circularly
        # (Optional: self.data[self.front] = 0 to clear the value, but not strictly needed)
        self.front = (self.front + 1) % self.capacity
        self.size -= 1
        return True

    def deleteLast(self) -> bool:
        """
        Deletes an item from the rear of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful.
        """
        if self.isEmpty():
            return False
        
        # Decrement rear pointer circularly to point to the actual last element
        self.rear = (self.rear - 1 + self.capacity) % self.capacity
        # (Optional: self.data[self.rear] = 0 to clear the value, but not strictly needed)
        self.size -= 1
        return True

    def getFront(self) -> int:
        """
        Get the front item from the deque.
        """
        if self.isEmpty():
            return -1
        return self.data[self.front]

    def getRear(self) -> int:
        """
        Get the last item from the deque.
        """
        if self.isEmpty():
            return -1
        # The rear pointer points to the *next available slot*, so we need to look at the element before it.
        return self.data[(self.rear - 1 + self.capacity) % self.capacity]

    def isEmpty(self) -> bool:
        """
        Checks whether the circular deque is empty or not.
        """
        return self.size == 0

    def isFull(self) -> bool:
        """
        Checks whether the circular deque is full or not.
        """
        return self.size == self.capacity


# Your MyCircularDeque object will be instantiated and called as such:
# obj = MyCircularDeque(k)

# param_2 = obj.insertLast(value)

# param_4 = obj.deleteLast()

# param_8 = obj.isFull()
